assertions.label: L1 submissionId and L2 identityHash share no value — rejection predicate satisfied
L1 record carries no uid input — rejection predicate is write-architecture, not policy
L2 record carries no blockHash or nonce — rejection predicate enforced on both lists
two-party threshold: single-authority rescission is structurally incomplete
participant-initiated withdrawal atomically removes both L1 and L2 records
swap-only replacement: L1 record is atomically replaced, L2 is unchanged — count-match preserved
action-category enumeration is closed — only disable/freeze/rescind/restore are valid
adverse-action rate limit: two rescissions on the same identity in the same period are structurally distinguishable
authority restoration: a previously revoked authority can be reinstated, producing a canonical restore event
referenced-action restoration: restore references the specific prior action ID it reverses
re-attestation audit record: re-attestation produces an append-only canonical log entry
submissionId is deterministic for same (blockHash, nonce)
different blockHashes produce different submissionIds — pre-computation impossible
different nonces produce different submissionIds — no reuse
submissionId contains no identity material — passes field-exclusivity test
beacon sliding window: stale block hash produces structurally different submissionId from fresh one
nonce-plus-payload committing identifier: payload hash is embedded in the identifier input
coercion_resistant defaultPosture → effectivePosture.writeOnly == true
recoverable defaultPosture + trusted signals → effectivePosture.recoverable == true
hostile_network signal + write_only_on_hostile_network:true → write-only, fallback reason recorded
web session approval gate: TTL-expired token produces structurally distinct state from valid token
identityHash = H(H(personId),scopeId) ≠ H(personId,scopeId) — IDV cannot derive on-chain hash
same personId, different scopings → different on-chain hashes — IDV cannot cross-correlate
browser write-only: BrowserClient struct carries no receipt-store field
IDV cast-count audit: each submission increments IDV counter independently of L1 record
IDV signed attestation log: each IDV attestation carries a scoping-id-scoped signature
recurring re-attestation sub-chain: each re-attestation references prior chain head
oracle-resistant sk_v binding: sk_v keypair generated on device; IDV never receives sk_v
canonical key-destruction attestation: sk_v destruction is a typed canonical event
P(id1 == id2 for two independent inputs) is negligible — formal non-derivability bound
SHA-256 output space is 2^256 — collision-resistance satisfies formal bound
timing-correlation bound: batch-flush ABCI sort removes per-record ordering metadata
|L1| = |L2| after N submissions — count-match universality
non-atomic write cannot satisfy count-match — rejection predicate enforced
validation-layer uniqueness: same (scopingId, identityHash) pair cannot appear twice in L2
ZK non-membership proof: nullifier F(sk,scopeId) ≠ H(uid,scopeId) — ZK mode structurally distinct
mapping-op exclusion: no system operation produces a (submissionId → identityHash) accumulator
intermediate-state non-materialization: batchCandidate struct is transient and never validator-addressable
batch-flush ordering: N submissions produce N independent submissionIds with no insertion-order correlation
independent derivation + temporal exclusion: submissionId shares no input with identityHash
passing uid as blockHash input does not reproduce identityHash — paths are disjoint
passing blockHash as uid input does not reproduce submissionId — paths are disjoint
cross-scoping unlinkability: same uid, different scopingIds → different identityHashes
cross-scoping: different uids, same scopingId → different identityHashes
sealed L2 attribute: sealing key is not present in the canonical L2 record
payload sealing: sealed L1 payload does not reveal direction — sealing key excluded from L1 record
store write-kernel: two-list invariant holds in store-backed anonymous submission
store write-only: receipt suppressed after write in write-only posture
store payload sealer: sealing key structurally excluded from canonical state
store L2 attribute sealing: both L1 payload and L2 identity attribute are sealed
credential-free rescission: biometric re-derivation enables rescission without memorized secrets
suppress/restore reveal path: suppressed reveal cannot be accessed until explicitly restored
wire write-kernel: two-list invariant holds for private value-transfer state machine
wire write-only: after transfer write, device retains no receipt linking sender/recipient to amount
wire L2 attribute sealing: recipient identity attribute sealed before L2 commit
account-control + enrollment gate: transfer only accepted from enrolled accounts
conservation audit surface: total supply conservation is publicly verifiable
wire two-party adverse action: freeze requires co-signature from two authorities
goal-gated financing: activation only triggers when cumulative remittance meets threshold
remittance nullifier uniqueness: each remittance ID is unique and non-reusable
per-contract parity attestation: each contract period produces a signed parity attestation
vote write-kernel: two-list invariant holds for eligible-participant anonymous-submission state machine
vote write-only: after ballot submission, device retains only direction-free ballot_id
partition migration (hostile-regime): migration increments SealedCount; device state is indistinguishable from write-only
write-only + partition migration: combined Claim 46 — SealedCount increment is sole canonical migration signal
sealed-partition cardinality counter: SealedCount is increment-only; global invariant preserved
four anomaly signals: missing-eligibility, duplicate-identity, count-mismatch, stale-beacon are structurally distinguishable
appeal + eligibility restoration: appeal produces typed event; eligibility restored after co-auth
domain-separator isolation: identity commitment for default tier uses distinct namespace prefix
identityHash is deterministic for same (uid, scopingId)
submissionId ≠ identityHash — no collision between L1 and L2 outputs
passing uid as blockHash input does not reproduce identityHash — paths are disjoint
passing blockHash as uid input does not reproduce submissionId — paths are disjoint
reconcile kernel: join key cannot be reconstructed through the reconcile interface
boolean-only presence surface: reconcile returns only true/false, not enumerable records
stateless invocations: reconcile does not accumulate cross-invocation state
fresh-input non-enumerability: reconcile requires fresh identity-derived input each invocation
authority-action audit surface: authority actions are logged to an append-only canonical surface
period-close attestation: HSM signs period-close over dual disjoint Merkle roots
rolling checkpoints: each checkpoint references prior checkpoint hash — append-only chain
store reconcile-kernel: join key cannot be reconstructed through store reconcile interface
reveal event record: reveal produces a typed canonical event with append-only commitment
non-bulk extraction gating: reconcile interface cannot return bulk record set
wire reconcile-kernel: wire reconcile cannot link transfer record to sender/recipient identity
conservation-aware forced redemption: redemption only proceeds when supply conservation is verified
freeze co-signature scoping: freeze scope is bound to a specific scoping identifier — no global freeze
vote reconcile-kernel: biometric IDV mandatory; ballot inclusion verifiable without disclosing direction
re-attestation status readback: participant receives matched/included status only — no direction revealed
rescission-evidence retrieval: participant receives direction-free rescission evidence only
reveal-evidence: requester receives direction-free ballot_id only; dual co-auth required; public canonical event
combined write + reconcile apparatus: write-side and reconcile-side are co-equal, structurally partitioned
two-party threshold authority rescission (system): system-level co-auth enforces rescission gate
multi-layer compositional cross-scoping: identity in scope A is unlinkable to identity in scope B at system level
optional payload field under posture control: payload field absent in write-only mode
posture-transition recorder: posture change produces canonical transition event
invocation non-repudiation log: reconcile invocations are logged with caller identity
commit authority partition: reconcile authority holds no commit key — partition enforced
HSM period-close attestation + dual Merkle: L1 root and L2 root are structurally disjoint
homomorphic per-option commitments: per-option tallies are publicly verifiable without revealing individual choices
CRM dual-mode deployment: write-kernel mode and reconcile-kernel mode are structurally distinct config states
k-of-n threshold enrollment: enrollment requires k-of-n credential factors before identity commitment is written
semi-write-only posture: CRM exposes direction-free receipt but not payload direction
VDF-hardened identifier: VDF output structurally distinct from plain SHA-256 beacon identifier
submissionId = H(blockHash,nonce) — direction is not an input
H(blockHash,'yes:'+nonce) ≠ H(blockHash,nonce) — direction-prefixed nonce not canonical
same uid, different scopingIds → different identityHashes
different uids, same scopingId → different identityHashes
H(uid || scopingId_A) ≠ H(uid) — scopingId is structurally required
submissionId derivation matches known SHA-256 answer — platform-neutral
identityHash derivation matches known SHA-256 answer — platform-neutral
identity commitment = SHA-256(namespace:provider:source) — 64-char hex, platform-neutral
real submission increments pendingRealCount — dummy slot will be absorbed
timer tick absorbs pending real slot — dummy not fired
timer tick with no pending real — dummy fires normally
N real submissions absorbed over N timer ticks — aggregate rate constant
dummy request is structurally indistinguishable in format from real request
dummy write never reaches canonical state — count-match invariant preserved
